title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Today, I'm going to show you how we spend our day here. Welcome to my crib.

That's my only complaint so far. Next, I'll show you the common room. There's a living room over here with couches and a TV. We all sit here and talk a lot at night. We've got the nice kitchen complete with Shin Ramen. Of course, my mother-in-law brought these just in case of an emergency. If we get tired of Vietnamese food, we can eat some Shin Ramen. And back here, there is no shower or bathtub. It's just a toilet. We call that a half bathroom. Without a doubt, the best part of this place is the private pool. There is even a fountain in this pool. It's just not turned on right now. The public pool closes early, but we can swim anytime we want in this pool. It's already super hot out, so I'm going to head to the public pool and beach area to get in the water and cool down a bit. Let's go. It is such a beautiful day today. And just look at the beautiful scenery here. Everything is just so green and fresh.

>> Hello. If I order a drink here, can it be brought to my chair over there? Okay, thank you. Can I please get one peach on the beach and one spicy royale? And that's it. I'm going to read my book while I wait for my cocktail. I just started this new fantasy novel. It's called The House in the Cerulean Sea.
It's a double decker bus. So, I'm excited. I'm boarding the bus. We're going to the top level.

>> We're on a guided tour now. Our guide is taking us to all of the best photo spots around the city. He is a professional photographer. I swear he's telling us how to pose and taking great photos of us. Look at these. We definitely got the shot. This is going to be my new profile picture.

title: will / shall
examples: I'll show you my bedroom first.

They have a pool table, a pingpong table, foosball, and they even have bowling there. But tonight, I am just way too tired. I'm going to change into my PJs and go to bed. Thanks for spending the day with me. I'll see you next time. Bye.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: >> I'm ready to swim, so I'm putting up my hair before I get in the water. I don't usually actually swim in the water. I just doggy paddle or float on a raft. I don't want to get my hair wet today, so I'm not gonna go under.
>> I'm trying to eat as much Vietnamese food as possible before we leave. It looks really delicious, and I love how they always serve it with fresh herbs on the side.

examples: The ceilings are so high. The bed is super comfortable. And we have a beautiful view of the pool. Wow. I think I can open this and dive into the pool from my bedroom. title: have to / must
examples: One more swim. Do we have time? It's tempting, but we have plans tonight. So, I have to go back to my room and try to get a little bit cleaned up.
I feel like a dog sticking its head out the window in the car. Duck. I have to duck because there are trees hitting the bus. Okay, I'm safe now. Oh, wow. It's like a city square.
